sourceMovie.title: Never Say Never Again
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Never Say Never Again for the same reason it broke through in 1983: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Irvin Kershner keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Skyfall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Never Say Never Again clicked for you, Skyfall (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Never Say Never Again?
faq.answer: Try Skyfall, Casino Royale, Spectre—each shares DNA with Never Say Never Again while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Never Say Never Again?
faq.answer: Never Say Never Again (1983) is best described as Adventure, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Never Say Never Again part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "James Bond Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
